reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own Q1-A answer (100 - 67) is correct. B and C have a perfect record on these simple arithmetic puzzles, and D has only made one early mistake across many games, with all later answers matching verification, so it’s very likely all four capsules are correct. That makes the chance the full password is wrong much smaller than the 25% death risk I’d face if nobody volunteers and a random player is killed. Verifying would just cost a coin without reducing this round’s death risk. So I volunteer now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own answer for Q2-B (33 + 27) is correct, and all current puzzles are simple arithmetic like in previous games. A and C have never missed on this type of problem, and D has only had one early mistake but has since been consistently correct over many rounds. The chance that any of these four basic answers is wrong is therefore much smaller than the 1-in-4 chance I die from random elimination if everyone only passes or verifies. Verifying would still leave me exposed to that random-death risk while also costing a coin, so the best move for both survival and coin preservation is to volunteer now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: I’m sure my own answer to Q3 (6 × 4) is correct, and A and B have a perfect record while D has only a single past mistake across many simple arithmetic puzzles. With exactly one capsule per question, there’s no visible conflict, so the chance the 4-part password is wrong is small but still nonzero.  

If I volunteer, I personally face that (small) error risk multiplied by the chance I’m the one selected to enter the password. If I pass, I only die if nobody at all volunteers and a random player is eliminated. So my death risk when passing is:  
- P(no one volunteers) × 1/4,  
whereas when volunteering it’s roughly:  
- (1 − q) × P(I’m the chosen volunteer).  

From prior games, someone (often A or B) has always stepped up immediately, so P(no volunteers) is plausibly much lower than the already small (1 − q). That makes passing safer for me than volunteering. Verifying would just cost a coin and mainly protects whoever volunteers, not me, so I skip it.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
✅ B entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
